title: encephalartos lebomboensis
intro: Encephalartos lebomboensis, also known as the Lebombo Cycad, is a species of cycad in the family Stangeriaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Swaziland. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, where its leaves and roots have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Encephalartos lebomboensis are said to include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating skin irritations, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a paste, although caution should be exercised when using any plant-based remedy.
